mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate pinned near the zero lower bound, the persistent low cost of capital keeps discount rates on equity cash flows compressed on an ongoing basis, providing a structural tailwind to S&P 500 returns over the next quarter regardless of short-term news flow.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns during ZLB periods are not significantly different from non-ZLB periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market healing that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 9.1 in mid-2011 to 8.3 by Feb 2012
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following triggered UNRATE declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in nonfarm payroll growth alongside a falling unemployment rate constitutes a stronger confirmation of labor market strength than either series alone, jointly signaling durable income growth that supports equity valuations over the next month; neither series in isolation reliably predicts this effect.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k AND UNRATE falling
failure_condition: The joint-condition signal's predictive power (Rank IC or hit rate) does not exceed the best single-series baseline by a statistically meaningful margin across a 2-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession housing recovery regime, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal renewed household investment and construction-linked employment, which compounds into broader demand growth and supports S&P 500 gains over a quarterly horizon; this effect is contingent on being in an early housing recovery, not a mature or declining housing cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 699k in early 2012, near post-2009 recovery lows rebounding
failure_condition: During periods of rising HOUST, 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive skew relative to unconditional baseline, or the effect reverses once housing starts exceed pre-recession trend levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month uptrend in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which forward-signals near-term corporate revenue strength and produces modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on positive INDPRO momentum show Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation crosses above a 3% threshold, markets begin pricing in an earlier-than-expected end to accommodative monetary policy, raising discount rate expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI threshold crossings are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or hit rate for negative returns is below 50% across the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with benign inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong real consumer spending power, and this joint condition of rising nominal sales with contained price growth signals genuine demand strength that supports S&P 500 gains over a quarterly horizon; rising RSAFS with simultaneously spiking CPI would instead reflect inflation-driven nominal effects without real strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND CPIAUCSL YoY < 3%
failure_condition: The joint real-retail-strength signal fails to outperform a naive RSAFS-only trend model in out-of-sample Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero lower bound regime coinciding with positive real GDP growth, the combination of ultra-low policy rates and expanding output signals that monetary accommodation is successfully translating into real economic momentum, producing a short-term positive S&P 500 reaction over the following week as markets price improved growth-without-tightening-risk dynamics.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% AND GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2%
regime_hint: zero lower bound recovery with positive growth
failure_condition: Conditional on both triggers being active, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive relative to unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across the available sample period.
